DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Barry Tagawa of The Law Office of Barry K. Tagawa telephone number 415-951-8600 for Plaintiff;
John Moura of Gilbert Kelly telephone number 213-615-7035 for Defendants.

Ruling: Counsel for the Defendants withdraws his contest to the demurrer. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RADIANCE OWNERS ASSOCIATION, MAYNARD/RICH MANAGEMENT COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA, CHRISTOPHER LECLERC, MATTHEW ECKMAN, MARGARET FAHNESTOCK, RICHARD DICKERSON and NANCY MADDEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT -
The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff correctly notes that the fiduciary duty is owed to the corporation and all shareholders. The confusion in the cause of action is that plaintiff is alleging derivative and individual claims. In Jones v. H.F. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal.3d 93, 106-107, the court explains: [T]he action is derivative, i.e., in the corporate right, if the gravamen of the complaint is injury to the corporation, or to the whole body of its stock and property without any severance or distribution among individual holders, or it seeks to recover assets for the corporation or to prevent the dissipation of its assets'The stockholder's individual suit, on the other hand, is a suit to enforce a right against the corporation which the stockholder possesses as an individual... If the injury is not incidental to an injury to the corporation, an individual cause of action exists. Here, plaintiff is mixing up corporate harms (not properly supervising MRM to the
detriment of Radiance, Amended Complaint 35:15-17) with individual harms (violating plaintiff's right to participate in the management of Radiance, Amended Complaint 35:20-24).
Plaintiff has leave to plead his individual harm more clearly. The demurrer to the 4th cause of action for negligence is overruled. Defendants owed a duty to plaintiff and pars. 115-116 allege severe emotional distress. In addition, even if plaintiff cannot recover emotional distress damages, par. 116 alleges other damages as well (e.g. lost wages). Defendants cannot demurrer to part of a cause of action. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is overruled. Plaintiff alleges severe emotional distress and there is a factual issue regarding whether defendant's conduct was outrageous. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/EG
